nodes.title: SAM3: what to track in the VIDEO
Track people in WHOLE video (one pass)
SCAIL-2 model (fp8 = half RAM; fp16 only if 64GB+ free)
Distill LoRA — BYPASSED for 6-char quality (re-enable + steps 6/cfg 1 for fast previews)
READ ME — 6-Character Quality Preset
widgets_values:
# 6-CHARACTER QUALITY PRESET

This preset is tuned for replacing up to 6 characters at once:
- **Quality mode is ON**: the Distill LoRA is bypassed, steps 40 / cfg 5. Turbo (cfg 1) collapses multi-identity binding to 1-2 replacements - do not re-enable it for final renders. Expect roughly 25-35 min per 81-frame chunk on an RTX 3090 at 896x512.
- For fast previews only: un-bypass the Distill LoRA (CTRL+B) and set steps 6 / cfg 1, then switch back before the real render.
- If some identities still hold on to the original person, use **multi-pass**: object_indices `0,1,2` on the Colored Mask node + first 3 refs -> render -> feed the output back as driving video for the remaining 3 (new cache_id per pass).
- Optional: raise WIDTH/HEIGHT to 1088x608 for more pixels per identity (slower; lower chunk_length if VRAM runs out).

---

# GAP SCAIL-2 Long Video — Multi-Character Replacement

Replaces **1–6 people** in a driving video (**any length**) with your reference characters. One queue press renders the whole video: the orchestrator slices it into chunks, generates each chunk anchored on the previous one, adapts the prompt per chunk, and stitches everything back together with the original audio.

## Quick start (follow the numbered groups)
1. **① SETTINGS** — set width/height once (multiples of 32).
2. **② DRIVING VIDEO** — load your source video.
3. **③ CHARACTERS** — load 1–6 character images.
4. Press **Run** — the first Run on a video is an **analysis pass** (generation skipped automatically): check **④ MASK CHECK** video, the **REF MASK preview** (③), **⑧ FOOTAGE ANALYSIS**, and follow the **NEXT STEP panel** in ⑥.
5. Fill the prompts on the **⑥ GENERATE** node (see the cheat-sheet note beside it).
6. Press **Run again** — rendering starts automatically (analysis is cached); the final video lands in **⑦ OUTPUT**.

## Identity colors (fixed by the model)
| # | color | replaces |
|---|-------|----------|
| 1 | blue | first/leftmost person to appear |
| 2 | red | second person |
| 3 | green | third |
| 4 | magenta / 5 cyan / 6 yellow | ... |

## Memory guide (RTX 3090 class)
- `chunk_length` **81** = trained value; drop to 65/49 if VRAM runs out.
- `vae_decode = tiled` removes the decode VRAM spike at high resolutions.
- System RAM: ~11 GB per 1000 source frames at 896×512.

## Crash / bad chunk?
- Crash or interrupt → set **cache_mode = resume** and queue again: finished chunks reload from disk and generation continues.
- One bad chunk → `chunk_rerender = <its number>` + new seed → only that chunk regenerates.

## Models
Download links for all models (fp8 recommended) are in the README: https://github.com/GeekatplayStudio/wan-scail2-gap — wan2.1_14B_SCAIL_2_fp8_scaled, DPO + lightx2v distill LoRAs, Wan2.1 VAE, umt5-xxl, clip_vision_h, sam3.1_multiplex.

## Troubleshooting
- **Wrong person replaced** → ④ note (object_indices remap).
- **Character replaced by colors only, body stays human** (e.g. toy bear) → set that character's SUBJECT text (③), check the REF MASK preview, disable turbo (steps 40 / cfg 5), lower pose_strength ≈ 0.7 and pose_end ≈ 0.8 — full recipe in the ⑥ cheat sheet.
- **Only 1-2 of several characters actually resemble their reference** → almost always a missing character_prompts line: EVERY detected character needs its own `N: description` line (copy the auto-generated CHARACTER PROMPTS TEMPLATE from ⑧) - missing ones now get a generic fallback automatically, but a real description works much better. Also check the report for a WARNING about identities with no reference view at all (wire character_count from the builder into the generator to enable this check).
- **System/RAM crash on long videos** → use the fp8 model (default; fp16 needs ~31 GB RAM alone), lower resolution, or split very long sources. After any crash set cache_mode = resume and queue again — finished chunks reload from disk.
- **Identity drifts when character turns** → add *GAP Character Extra View* (③ note).
- **Ghosting at a camera cut** → lower `scene_cut_threshold` so the cut is detected.
- **Want a different take of one chunk** → cache_mode `resume` + `chunk_rerender` + new seed.

---
Workflow by **Geekatplay Studio** — tutorials: [youtube.com/@geekatplay](https://www.youtube.com/@geekatplay)

## Log noise
`ConnectionResetError [WinError 10054]` lines in the console are harmless — that's the browser tab reconnecting its websocket, not a workflow error. If the log says `Prompt executed`, the run succeeded.

nodes.title: MASK CHECK: verify who got which color (1=blue, 2=red...)
① Resolution — set ONCE
widgets_values:
### Resolution (single source)
These two values feed the video resize, the character reference builder and the generator — nothing else to keep in sync.

- Must be **multiples of 32**.
- `896×512` = landscape default; swap to `512×896` for portrait video.
- Lower resolution = less VRAM + faster; SCAIL-2 works well around 0.5 MP.

nodes.title: ④ Tracking & mask check
widgets_values:
### People tracking
SAM3 tracks every person across the **whole video in one pass**, so identity colors stay consistent across all chunks. The text prompt (default `human`) selects what to track — make it more specific in crowded scenes (e.g. `man in a suit`).

### MASK CHECK video (saved on queue)
Identity colors blended over the footage — **verify who got which color before generating**: 1=blue, 2=red, 3=green, 4=magenta, 5=cyan, 6=yellow.

**Wrong assignment?** Set `object_indices` on the *Create SCAIL-2 Colored Mask* node:
- `1,0` → swap characters 1 and 2
- `0,2` → keep persons 0 and 2, skip person 1 (not replaced)

nodes.title: ③ Characters
widgets_values:
### Character images
One clear image per character (portrait OK - images are fit, never cropped). *SAM3 Detect* masks the subject using the **SUBJECT text under each image** - set it to what the image contains: person, toy bear, robot...

With 2+ characters the builder composites all cutouts onto **one primary reference** (like the group photo the model expects) and sends ONLY that - the model was trained with a single reference frame, and stacking one view per character degrades placement and causes distortion. Keep `individual_views` OFF.

**Check the REF MASK preview** below the builder: the composite must show every character in its color (1=blue, 2=red, ...). Black/partial = SAM3 missed it -> fix the SUBJECT text. **The colors must pair with the same people in the MASK CHECK video** - if not, reorder your images or use object_indices.

- Character 1 replaces the first/leftmost person in the video.
- More characters (max 6): duplicate LoadImage + SUBJECT + Detect, wire to image_3/mask_3.
- Stronger identity: add *GAP Character Extra View* between builder and generator.

nodes.title: ⑥ Generator options — cheat sheet
widgets_values:
### Prompts
- **base_prompt** — the scene/style; put `{characters}` where character descriptions go.
- **character_prompts** — one line per character: `1: a man in a red jacket…`. Injected automatically **only in chunks where that character is on screen**.
- **prompt_schedule** — per-frame-range actions: `0-151: they dance`. Get a pre-filled template from ⑧ FOOTAGE ANALYSIS. Empty ranges fall back to base_prompt. `#` lines are comments.

### Chunking / memory
- **chunk_length** 81 (trained). Lower → less VRAM. **overlap** 5 = frames carried between chunks.
- **vae_decode** `tiled` → much lower VRAM during decode.

### Scene cuts
- **detect_scene_cuts** on: chunk boundaries snap to hard cuts, anchor resets at each cut (no ghosting). **scene_cut_threshold** lower = more sensitive.

### New render vs resume
- **cache_mode** `new render` (default) = every queue renders fresh; finished chunks are still checkpointed to disk as you go.
- Crash / interrupt? Switch to `resume` and queue → continues from the last finished chunk.
- **chunk_rerender** (needs `resume`): `3` or `2,5` or `4-6` + a new seed → regenerate only those chunks. **rerender_cascade** also redoes everything after (perfect continuity).
- One **cache_id** per project; if the video/settings changed, resume detects it and safely starts fresh.

### Speed / quality
- Turbo (default): distill LoRA on, **steps 6, cfg 1**.
- Max quality: bypass the *Distill LoRA* node (CTRL+B) in ⑤ MODELS, set **steps 40, cfg 5**.
- **seed**: per-chunk seed = seed + chunk number.

### Sync & color
- **pad_to_source_length** on: output matches source frame count (audio stays in sync).
- **color_match** `lab`: cancels color drift between chunks.

### Live progress
The node shows `chunk i/N | frames | characters` while working; the console logs the full plan and every prompt. The **report** output (⑦) is the final per-chunk summary.

### Non-human characters (toy bear, robot, creature…)
If the output only picks up colors but keeps a human body:
1. Set the character's **SUBJECT text** (group ③) to what it is — e.g. `pink plush teddy bear` — and confirm the REF MASK preview shows its full silhouette.
2. Describe it explicitly in **character_prompts**: `1: a fluffy pink plush teddy bear with round ears and stubby arms`.
3. **Turn off turbo** — cfg 1 barely follows prompts. Bypass the *Distill LoRA* (⑤, CTRL+B) and set **steps 40, cfg 5**.
4. Loosen the pose so the human body shape isn't forced: **pose_strength ≈ 0.7**, **pose_end ≈ 0.8** (lower further for very different proportions).

### Characters do not resemble the references?
1. Best-quality mode: bypass the Distill LoRA (5, CTRL+B), **steps 40, cfg 5** - turbo (cfg 1) costs identity fidelity.
2. Use clean, well-lit, front-facing reference photos; add a *GAP Character Extra View* (back view / close-up) per character.
3. Check the REF MASK preview - a partial mask means only part of the character reaches the model.
4. Higher resolution (e.g. 1088x608) gives the model more pixels per face.

### character_prompts: every detected character needs a line
Any detected character WITHOUT a line in character_prompts now gets an automatic
generic fallback description instead of silently getting none (this used to cause
weak/no resemblance for uncaptioned characters). Still, write a real description
per character for best resemblance - copy the auto-generated template from ⑧ (CHARACTER PROMPTS TEMPLATE panel).

### character_count input (top-left of this node)
Wired from the reference builder. If the driving video has MORE tracked identities
than reference characters you loaded, the report/log will WARN you by name/color -
those extra identities have no reference view and will not be replaced. Fix by
loading more character images, or excluding the extra person via object_indices on
the Colored Mask node.

nodes.title: ⑧ Footage analysis — phase 1
widgets_values:
### Analyze before you render (automatic)
The **PHASE GATE** (top of ⑥) defaults to `auto`:

1. **Run 1** → tracking, MASK CHECK video and this analysis run; generation is skipped. The **NEXT STEP** panel under the gate tells you exactly what to review.
2. Copy **schedule_template** into **prompt_schedule**, fill in the actions; check masks.
3. Copy **CHARACTER PROMPTS TEMPLATE** (new panel below) into **character_prompts** on the generator, then edit each line to describe what that reference image actually shows. Every detected character needs its own line - one left out gets only a generic fallback description (still works, but a real description resembles the reference much better).
4. **Run 2** (just press Run again) → rendering starts; analysis is cached.

New video loaded → the next Run is an analysis pass again, automatically.
Force behavior anytime: phase `1` = always analyze, `2` = always render.

nodes.title: REF MASK CHECK: composite primary + one color per character
>>> NEXT STEP — read this after every Run <<<
CHARACTER PROMPTS TEMPLATE - copy into character_prompts, then edit
Schedule template -> copy into prompt_schedule, fill actions
PHASE GATE — Run 1: analyze | Run 2: render (auto)

widgets_values: Cinematic footage of the scene from the driving video. {characters} Natural motion, consistent lighting, high detail, no text, no watermarks, no camera cuts.

# Auto-generated character_prompts template - copy this into the generator's
# character_prompts field, then EDIT each line: replace 'the subject from
# reference image N' with what that reference actually shows (species,
# clothing, distinguishing features). Every detected character needs its own
# line here, or it renders with only a generic fallback description.
1: The first character is the subject from reference image 1 (blue), fully recognizable and closely matching that reference image.
2: The second character is the subject from reference image 2 (red), fully recognizable and closely matching that reference image.
3: The third character is the subject from reference image 3 (green), fully recognizable and closely matching that reference image.
4: The fourth character is the subject from reference image 4 (magenta), fully recognizable and closely matching that reference image.
5: The fifth character is the subject from reference image 5 (cyan), fully recognizable and closely matching that reference image.
6: The sixth character is the subject from reference image 6 (yellow), fully recognizable and closely matching that reference image.

# Auto-generated schedule template - fill the action after each range.
# Lines starting with # are ignored; ranges left empty fall back to base_prompt.
# frames 0-459 (0.0s-15.3s) | visible: character 1 (blue) + character 2 (red) + character 3 (green) + character 4 (magenta) + character 5 (cyan) + character 6 (yellow)
0-459:
nodes.title: GENERATE — 6 characters, QUALITY MODE (steps 40 / cfg 5)
Character timeline (how many refs you need)

groups.title: ⑥ GENERATE — Run 1 analyzes, Run 2 renders (auto phase)
⑧ FOOTAGE ANALYSIS (optional dry-run: mute ⑥, queue once)
